channel.title: Redwan Ahmed Utsab
channel.description: Recent content on Md Zarif Ul Alam

item.description: The Impossible List The Impossible List is NOT a Bucket List 🪣. A bucket list is made up of someday dreams, the impossible list requires action today. A bucket list is focused on the event, the impossible list is focused on the journey (that’s why you will find me writing about the current state of the list items below). Professional / Academic ✔️ Contribute to an open source software project

Projects Highlights of some of my projects, mostly done during my undergrad. For a complete list of my projects, please visit my GitHub Ray Tracing August 2022 Deep Dive into the 3D world with RTX-ON Implemented a ray tracing pipeline from scratch. Phong Lighting Model and Recursive Relfection is used for proper lighting and ray tracing. 🏷️ Computer Graphics IntDesk August 2022 A tech interview preparation platform IntDesk is an all-in-one tech interview preperation platform.

item.title: Awards and Achievements 🏅
item.description: AI for Bangla 2.0 June 2023 🏅 Our team “Silent Sentinels” got honourable mention at the AI for Bangla 2.0 research competition! 🏷️ Competition Research Machine Learning 🔗 News Bangla Complex Named Entity Recognition Challenge January 2023 🏆 Champion of the Bangla Complex Named Entity Recognition Challenge. It was organized as part of Bangladesh’s first NLP Hackathon by Bengali Language Enrichment in Information Technology through Research and Development Project (EBLICT) and Bangladesh Open Source Network (BDOSN), and supported by ICT Division Bangladesh, Bangladesh Computer Council (BCC) and Amazon Web Services (AWS).

item.title: Experience 👨🏻‍💻
item.description: Center for Intelligent Information Retrieval, UMass Amherst Research Assistant Sept. '24 ~ Present Broadly working on multimodal information retrieval Currently focusing on knowledge-intensive video question answering Chaldal Ltd. Machine Learning Engineer - L3 (Specialist) July '24 ~ Aug. '24 Worked on further improving the perishable prediction algorithm by incorporating probability distributions and uncertainty estimation. Laid the groundwork for personalized recommendations and marketing for the Chaldal platform. Created Superset dashboards to monitor real-time performance of the ML models deployed in production, enabling quick identification and resolution of issues.

item.title: News 🗞️
item.description: 🗓️ Jan, 2025 🎩 Received the Edward Riseman and Allen Hanson Scholarship from UMass CICS! 🏷️ Academic 🗓️ July, 2024 📈 Got promoted again to Machine Learning Engineer (Level 3 Specialist) at Chaldal! 🏷️ Career 🗓️ June, 2024 🧳 Awarded travel grant to attend MICCAI 2024 at Marrakesh, Morocco! 🏷️ Academic 🗓️ March, 2024 🎩 End of admission season! Will be starting my PhD in CS at UMass Amherst from Fall ‘24!

item.title: Paper Towns 🗞️
item.description: A paper a day keeps the mind awake! A collection of interesting papers I read, and my summary/thoughts on them. The author name mentioned is generally the author by whom I was introduced to the paper. Otherwise, the first author is mentioned. Large language models are zero-shot rankers for recommender systems Julian McAuley (UCSD) et al. European Conference on Information Retrieval (ECIR) 2024 Implicit Chain of Thought Reasoning via Knowledge Distillation Yuntian Deng (UWaterloo) et al.

item.title: Research 🔍
item.description: My research interest is in the intersection of Natural Language Processing and Computer Vision. Particularly, I am interested in multi-modal learning, language grounding and low-resource machine learning. Multi-Modal Retrieval Augmentation for Open-Ended and Knowledge-Intensive Video Question Answering Sept. 2024 ‑ Present 🟡 Working on improving Knowledge-Intensive Video Question Answering using Multi-Modal Retrieval Augmentation Collaborators: Hamed Zamani (Associate Professor, CICS, UMass Amherst) 🏷️ Vision Language Models Retrieval Augmented Generation Video Question Answering
